pattern: Walk each digit of the number and test divisibility directly against the original value; a digit's divisibility only depends on its own value, never its position.
hints: Look at each digit of the number one at a time and just check whether it evenly divides the whole number.
You can get the digits either by converting to a string or by repeatedly taking num % 10 and dividing by 10 -- both visit every digit exactly once.
Since every digit here is 1-9 (no zero digit ever appears), you never need to guard against dividing by zero.

edge_inputs.description: the smallest possible input, a single digit of one
the largest all-nines value once the no-zero-digit rule applies
neither digit happens to divide the number
one digit divides evenly while the other does not
a repeated identical digit multiplies the divisible count
a single-digit number always divides itself

stress.description: random numbers up to the full range with no zero digit
